root_text.an10.85:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā mahācundo cetīsu viharati sahajātiyaṁ.
root_text.an10.85:1.2: Tatra kho āyasmā mahācundo bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.an10.85:1.4: “Āvuso”ti kho te bhikkhū āyasmato mahācundassa paccassosuṁ.
root_text.an10.85:2.2: ‘ahaṁ paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjāmipi vuṭṭhahāmipi, ahaṁ dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjāmipi vuṭṭhahāmipi, ahaṁ tatiyaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjāmipi vuṭṭhahāmipi, ahaṁ catutthaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjāmipi vuṭṭhahāmipi, ahaṁ ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ samāpajjāmipi vuṭṭhahāmipi, ahaṁ viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ samāpajjāmipi vuṭṭhahāmipi, ahaṁ ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ samāpajjāmipi vuṭṭhahāmipi, ahaṁ nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ samāpajjāmipi vuṭṭhahāmipi, ahaṁ saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ samāpajjāmipi vuṭṭhahāmipī’ti.
root_text.an10.85:3.1: Tamenaṁ tathāgato vā tathāgatasāvako vā jhāyī samāpattikusalo paracittakusalo paracittapariyāyakusalo samanuyuñjati samanuggāhati samanubhāsati.
root_text.an10.85:3.2: So tathāgatena vā tathāgatasāvakena vā jhāyinā samāpattikusalena paracittakusalena paracittapariyāyakusalena samanuyuñjiyamāno samanuggāhiyamāno samanubhāsiyamāno irīṇaṁ āpajjati vicinaṁ āpajjati anayaṁ āpajjati byasanaṁ āpajjati anayabyasanaṁ āpajjati.
root_text.an10.85:4.1: Tamenaṁ tathāgato vā tathāgatasāvako vā jhāyī samāpattikusalo paracittakusalo paracittapariyāyakusalo evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca manasi karoti:
root_text.an10.85:4.2: ‘kiṁ nu kho ayamāyasmā katthī hoti vikatthī adhigamesu—
root_text.an10.85:5.1: Tamenaṁ tathāgato vā tathāgatasāvako vā jhāyī samāpattikusalo paracittakusalo paracittapariyāyakusalo evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāti:
root_text.an10.85:6.1: ‘Dīgharattaṁ kho ayamāyasmā khaṇḍakārī chiddakārī sabalakārī kammāsakārī na santatakārī na santatavutti sīlesu.
root_text.an10.85:6.3: Dussilyaṁ kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.85:7.2: assaddhiyaṁ kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.85:8.2: appasaccaṁ kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.85:9.2: dovacassatā kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.85:10.2: pāpamittatā kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.85:11.2: kosajjaṁ kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.85:12.2: muṭṭhassaccaṁ kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.85:13.2: kohaññaṁ kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.85:14.2: dubbharatā kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.85:15.2: duppaññatā kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ’.
root_text.an10.85:16.1: Seyyathāpi, āvuso, sahāyako sahāyakaṁ evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.an10.85:16.2: ‘yadā te, samma, dhanena dhanakaraṇīyaṁ assa, yāceyyāsi maṁ dhanaṁ. Dassāmi te dhanan’ti.
root_text.an10.85:16.3: So kiñcideva dhanakaraṇīye samuppanne sahāyako sahāyakaṁ evaṁ vadeyya: ‘attho me, samma, dhanena. Dehi me dhanan’ti.
root_text.an10.85:16.5: ‘tena hi, samma, idha khanāhī’ti. So tatra khananto nādhigaccheyya.
root_text.an10.85:16.7: ‘alikaṁ maṁ, samma, avaca; tucchakaṁ maṁ, samma, avaca—idha khanāhī’ti.
root_text.an10.85:16.9: ‘nāhaṁ taṁ, samma, alikaṁ avacaṁ, tucchakaṁ avacaṁ. Tena hi, samma, idha khanāhī’ti.
root_text.an10.85:16.12: ‘alikaṁ maṁ, samma, avaca, tucchakaṁ maṁ, samma, avaca—idha khanāhī’ti.
root_text.an10.85:16.14: ‘nāhaṁ taṁ, samma, alikaṁ avacaṁ, tucchakaṁ avacaṁ. Tena hi, samma, idha khanāhī’ti.
root_text.an10.85:16.17: ‘alikaṁ maṁ, samma, avaca, tucchakaṁ maṁ, samma, avaca—idha khanāhī’ti.
root_text.an10.85:16.19: ‘nāhaṁ taṁ, samma, alikaṁ avacaṁ, tucchakaṁ avacaṁ. Api ca ahameva ummādaṁ pāpuṇiṁ cetaso vipariyāyan’ti.
root_text.an10.85:17.1: Evamevaṁ kho, āvuso, bhikkhu katthī hoti vikatthī adhigamesu:
root_text.an10.85:17.2: ‘ahaṁ paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjāmipi vuṭṭhahāmipi, ahaṁ dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjāmipi vuṭṭhahāmipi, ahaṁ tatiyaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjāmipi vuṭṭhahāmipi, ahaṁ catutthaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjāmipi vuṭṭhahāmipi, ahaṁ ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ samāpajjāmipi vuṭṭhahāmipi, ahaṁ viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ samāpajjāmipi vuṭṭhahāmipi, ahaṁ ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ samāpajjāmipi vuṭṭhahāmipi, ahaṁ nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ samāpajjāmipi vuṭṭhahāmipi, ahaṁ saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ samāpajjāmipi vuṭṭhahāmipī’ti.
root_text.an10.85:18.1: Tamenaṁ tathāgato vā tathāgatasāvako vā jhāyī samāpattikusalo paracittakusalo paracittapariyāyakusalo samanuyuñjati samanuggāhati samanubhāsati.
root_text.an10.85:18.2: So tathāgatena vā tathāgatasāvakena vā jhāyinā samāpattikusalena paracittakusalena paracittapariyāyakusalena samanuyuñjiyamāno samanuggāhiyamāno samanubhāsiyamāno irīṇaṁ āpajjati vicinaṁ āpajjati anayaṁ āpajjati byasanaṁ āpajjati anayabyasanaṁ āpajjati.
root_text.an10.85:19.1: Tamenaṁ tathāgato vā tathāgatasāvako vā jhāyī samāpattikusalo paracittakusalo paracittapariyāyakusalo evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca manasi karoti:
root_text.an10.85:19.2: ‘kiṁ nu kho ayamāyasmā katthī hoti vikatthī adhigamesu—
root_text.an10.85:20.1: Tamenaṁ tathāgato vā tathāgatasāvako vā jhāyī samāpattikusalo paracittapariyāyakusalo cetasā ceto paricca pajānāti:
root_text.an10.85:21.1: ‘Dīgharattaṁ kho ayamāyasmā khaṇḍakārī chiddakārī sabalakārī kammāsakārī, na santatakārī na santatavutti sīlesu.
root_text.an10.85:21.3: dussilyaṁ kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.85:22.2: assaddhiyaṁ kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.85:23.2: appasaccaṁ kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.85:24.2: dovacassatā kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.85:25.2: pāpamittatā kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.85:26.2: kosajjaṁ kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.85:27.2: muṭṭhassaccaṁ kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.85:28.2: kohaññaṁ kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.85:29.2: dubbharatā kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.85:30.2: duppaññatā kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ’.
root_text.an10.85:31.1: So vatāvuso, bhikkhu ‘ime dasa dhamme appahāya imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissatī’ti netaṁ ṭhānaṁ vijjati.
root_text.an10.85:31.2: So vatāvuso, bhikkhu ‘ime dasa dhamme pahāya imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissatī’ti ṭhānametaṁ vijjatī”ti.

translation_text.an10.85:1.1: At one time Venerable Mahācunda was staying in the land of the Cetīs at Sahajāti.
translation_text.an10.85:2.1: “Take a mendicant who boasts and brags about their achievements:
translation_text.an10.85:2.2: ‘I enter and emerge from the first absorption, the second absorption, the third absorption, and the fourth absorption. And I enter and emerge from the dimensions of infinite space, infinite consciousness, nothingness, and neither perception nor non-perception. And I enter and emerge from the cessation of perception and feeling.’
translation_text.an10.85:3.1: They’re pursued, pressed, and grilled by the Realized One, or by one of his disciples who has the absorptions, and is skilled in attainments, in the minds of others, and in encompassing another’s mind.
translation_text.an10.85:3.2: Grilled in this way they get stuck or lose their way. They fall to ruin and disaster.
translation_text.an10.85:4.1: The Realized One or one of his disciples encompasses their mind and investigates:
translation_text.an10.85:4.2: ‘Why does this venerable boast and brag about their achievements, saying,
translation_text.an10.85:4.3: “I enter and emerge from the first absorption …
translation_text.an10.85:4.4: and the cessation of perception and feeling.”’
translation_text.an10.85:6.1: ‘For a long time this venerable’s deeds have been broken, tainted, spotty, and marred. Their deeds and behavior are inconsistent.
translation_text.an10.85:6.3: and unethical conduct means decline in the teaching and training proclaimed by the Realized One.
translation_text.an10.85:7.2: and lack of faith means decline …
translation_text.an10.85:8.1: This venerable is unlearned and unpracticed,
translation_text.an10.85:8.2: and lack of learning means decline …
translation_text.an10.85:9.1: This venerable is hard to admonish,
translation_text.an10.85:9.2: and being hard to admonish means decline …
translation_text.an10.85:15.2: and lack of wisdom means decline in the teaching and training proclaimed by the Realized One.’
translation_text.an10.85:16.1: Suppose one friend was to say to another:
translation_text.an10.85:16.2: ‘My dear, when you need money for some payment, just ask me and I’ll give it.’
translation_text.an10.85:16.3: Then when some payment falls due, that friend says to their friend: ‘I need some money, my dear. Give me some.’
translation_text.an10.85:16.5: ‘Well then, my dear, dig here.’ So they dig there, but don’t find anything.
translation_text.an10.85:16.7: ‘You lied to me, my dear, you spoke hollow words when you told me to dig here.’
translation_text.an10.85:16.9: ‘My dear, I didn’t lie or speak hollow words. Well then, dig here.’
translation_text.an10.85:16.10: So they dig there as well, but don’t find anything.
translation_text.an10.85:16.12: ‘You lied to me, my dear, you spoke hollow words when you said dig here.’
translation_text.an10.85:16.14: ‘My dear, I didn’t lie or speak hollow words. Well then, dig here.’
translation_text.an10.85:16.15: So they dig there as well, but don’t find anything.
translation_text.an10.85:16.17: ‘You lied to me, my dear, you spoke hollow words when you said dig here.’
translation_text.an10.85:16.19: ‘My dear, I didn’t lie or speak hollow words. But I had gone mad, I was out of my mind.’
translation_text.an10.85:17.1: In the same way, take a mendicant who boasts and brags about their achievements:
translation_text.an10.85:17.2: ‘I enter and emerge from the first absorption … and the cessation of perception and feeling.’
translation_text.an10.85:18.1: They’re pursued, pressed, and grilled by the Realized One, or by one of his disciples …
translation_text.an10.85:18.2: Grilled in this way they get stuck or lose their way. They fall to ruin and disaster.
translation_text.an10.85:19.1: The Realized One or one of his disciples encompasses their mind and investigates:
translation_text.an10.85:19.2: ‘Why does this venerable boast and brag about their achievements, saying,
translation_text.an10.85:19.3: “I enter and emerge from the first absorption …
translation_text.an10.85:19.4: and the cessation of perception and feeling.”’
translation_text.an10.85:21.1: ‘For a long time this venerable’s deeds have been broken, tainted, spotty, and marred. Their deeds and behavior are inconsistent.
translation_text.an10.85:21.3: and unethical conduct means decline in the teaching and training proclaimed by the Realized One.
translation_text.an10.85:30.2: and lack of wisdom means decline in the teaching and training proclaimed by the Realized One.’
translation_text.an10.85:31.1: It is quite impossible for a mendicant to achieve growth, improvement, or maturity in this teaching and training without giving up these ten qualities.
translation_text.an10.85:31.2: It is quite possible for a mendicant to achieve growth, improvement, or maturity in this teaching and training after giving up these ten qualities.”

variant_text.an10.85:16.2: dhanena → bandho (mr) | assa, yāceyyāsi maṁ → yācissasi maṁ (bj, pts1ed); pavedeyyāsi maṁ (sya-all); parājeyyāpi maṁ (mr)
